response.justification: Bogdan is a highly accomplished full-stack and high-performance engineer with an impressive trajectory through Meta and Google, but the role's core requirement — deep, sustained C++ expertise — is essentially absent from his profile. His only C++ exposure is a six-month stint at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a university thesis project; his primary languages are TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. Beyond the language mismatch, the role demands familiarity with IEC 62443 industrial cybersecurity, MQTT, industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, etc.), and SDL practices — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. The advantageous skills he does possess (React, Node.js, event-driven architecture) are secondary to the position's hard requirements.
strengths: Demonstrated high-performance, low-latency engineering with DLang at Sociomantic, handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O and concurrent primitives — transferable mindset to C++ performance work.
Strong event-driven and distributed-systems background across multiple roles, aligning with the message-broker and large-scale application aspects of the job.
Proven seniority and quality mindset evidenced by Staff Engineer roles at Google and Meta, along with a history of TDD advocacy, mentoring, and technical leadership.
concerns: C++ experience is negligible — approximately six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis; the role requires 8–12 years of C++ as the primary language.
No mention of IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle (SDL), or any industrial cybersecurity practice, which are explicit non-negotiable competencies for this position.
Zero exposure to industrial automation protocols (Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0) or MQTT, and no IoT or industrial-domain background beyond a GIS mapping platform.
